title: Author filter uses LIKE substring match rather than exact equality
evidence: app.py:99 SELECT * FROM books WHERE author LIKE '%'||?||'%' — GET /books?author=Fitzgerald matches 'F. Scott Fitzgerald'
suggestion: Acceptable; note that partial matches are returned. Use exact match if strict filtering is intended.

title: init_db() runs at import time and writes books.db into the workspace
evidence: app.py:200 calls init_db() at module load; a 12KB books.db was committed into the run archive
suggestion: Guard DB init behind __main__ or an app factory so importing the module (e.g. in tests) does not create a stray production DB file.

title: PUT allows blank-string fields to be rejected but does not distinguish absent vs null
evidence: app.py:153-156 uses data.get('title', row['title']) so omitted fields are preserved; explicit null would fall through to validation
suggestion: Behavior is reasonable for partial updates; documenting the null-vs-omitted semantics would help API consumers.
